selected_goal: Rank suspicious rendered-crop dimension evidence by severity and expose compact review candidates in the output-integrity audit.
why_this_now: Iteration 4 added summary buckets, but failure examples still come from record order. Auditors should see the most severe tall/aspect-ratio crops first, especially when example_limit hides the full list.
repo_evidence: .agent-runs/2026-06-19T08-10-11-928Z/iteration-04/audit.current.integrity.json shows max-height and max-aspect candidates, but the failure examples still start with record-order candidates.
.agent-loop/project-gates.md says crop-related work needs sampled output evidence; ranked candidates make the sample deterministic and high-priority.
src/exam_bank/audit.py already has dimensions and reasons needed for deterministic severity sorting.
acceptance_criteria: Suspicious rendered-crop failure examples are sorted by severity before example_limit is applied.
The audit report includes suspicious_rendered_crop_review_candidates with compact top candidates.
Ranking is deterministic using reason count, height, aspect ratio, and path tie-breakers.
test_expectations: Extend the suspicious-dimension test to assert the taller crop appears first in examples and review candidates.
implementation_boundaries: Do not copy images or create a gallery in this iteration.
Do not modify generated output artifacts.
repo_hygiene_risk: Low. This is a report-ordering and compact evidence change in the existing audit.
rollback_plan: Remove the ranking helper, review_candidates field, and added assertions.
done_when: Targeted tests pass and the live audit report exposes top suspicious crop candidates in severity order.
